accepted.reason: Timedelta component extraction over dense int64 arrays is a practical data-processing kernel with predictable loop structure.
Board-neighborhood move enumeration is a compact combinatorial search kernel with clear constraints.
Bitboard directional scans and mobility counting are high-signal bitwise optimization kernels.
Open-address linear probing with growth/rehash captures core hash-table performance logic.
Shifted-window Pearson correlation is a useful image-analysis reduction kernel.
Owner/local-global index transforms provide concise distributed-array mapping primitives.
Roche potential and effective-gravity computations are rich numeric kernels with physics-oriented structure.
Optimized Mandelbrot grid evaluation is a classic complex-iteration workload with heavy inner loops.

skipped.reason: Heavily coupled to project-specific geometry utilities and environment attributes; lower standalone portability.
Primarily a C/CUDA wrapper and object binding layer rather than direct standalone computational kernel logic.
